# passwork_item (Resource)

Manages a Passwork password item.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "passwork_vault" "main" {
  name = "My Vault"
}

resource "passwork_folder" "infra" {
  name     = "Infrastructure"
  vault_id = passwork_vault.main.id
}

resource "passwork_item" "example" {
  name        = "PostgreSQL prod"
  vault_id    = passwork_vault.main.id
  folder_id   = passwork_folder.infra.id
  login       = "postgres"
  password    = var.db_password
  url         = "postgresql://prod.db.example.com:5432/mydb"
  description = "Production PostgreSQL credentials"
  tags        = ["database", "production"]
  color_code  = 1

  custom_fields = [
    {
      name  = "Port"
      type  = "text"
      value = "5432"
    },
    {
      name  = "SSL Mode"
      type  = "text"
      value = "require"
    },
  ]
}

variable "db_password" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

# Import existing item:
# terraform import passwork_item.example <item-id>
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the item.
- `vault_id` (String) ID of the vault this item belongs to.

### Optional

- `custom_fields` (Attributes List) List of custom fields. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--custom_fields))
- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `folder_id` (String) ID of the folder this item belongs to. Omit for vault root.
- `login` (String) Username / login.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password value. Stored in Passwork with client-side encryption when the master password is configured.
- `tags` (List of String) List of tags.
- `url` (String) Associated URL.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Item identifier.

<a id="nestedatt--custom_fields"></a>
### Nested Schema for `custom_fields`

Required:

- `name` (String) Field name.
- `type` (String) Field type (e.g. `text`, `password`).
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Field value.
